reviews.title: These saved my ears! Literally.
reviews.text: My new Bose 700 headphones kept causing an allergic reaction to my ears and the skin all the way around my ears.  Like blisters, redness, swelling and SEVERE itching.  The first time, I wore them for an afternoon and it took 3 weeks for the allergy to be cleared up.  I then washed the cups of the headphones with a soapy wash rag and rinsed well.  The second time I wore them for just under 2 hours and again had a reaction that took 2 weeks to clear up.  I use another brand of headphones daily to watch TV (that I also purchased from amazon) and have never had a reaction before.  I found these covers and they have completely fixed the reaction issue.  I also ordered another brand of ear cup covers off amazon to try but prefer these.  I'll be ordering another so I'll have extras just in case 😉.

reviews.title: Does what i expect it to do
reviews.text:
I expect it to keep the sweat off the ear pads, and it does this.

But since it wraps over the ear cups, it does not allow the ear cups to rest nicely around your ears.
This means that it affects the headset's noise cancellation a bit.

Also, if you wear it for a prolonged amount of time, it hurts the ears and is less comfortable. I wear glasses and this exacerbates the tension behind my ears.
This is particularly for me with my glasses.

reviews.title: Reasonably comfy, but poor fabric quality and small sound hole.
reviews.text:
06/22/22. Revised rating down to 1-star. The fabric is awful, pills badly and attracts every bit of dust and hair. I stopped using these on Zoom calls because they look disgusting. See photo. This is what yours will look like after a few months.

----------------------

Fits my Yamaha MT5 headphones fine. It may make zero difference in sound, but I feel like the center hole is about half the size it should be. Not sure what they were thinking. I'd like to see to somebody make something similar, with thicker, more breathable fabric, and 1-inch hole.

reviews.title: Very big, but useful.
reviews.text: Very large, I was hoping for a snug fit. These extended further than the actual cushion.  So if you want to charge you will need to take off or just pull off a little. Still useful as I was tired of white peel off all over my skin and clothes. But definitely recommend for a bigger headphone.

Sound quality is good and mic quality isn't bad, either.  After wearing them for an hour, my ears really need a break, though - but I have a pretty big head; these should be more comfortable for my middle schoolers.  I wish they came with a blue accent as an option, though.  The ones at school will have the green accents.

description: Meet the aptly named true wireless earbud for every part of your daily grind. Say hello to Grind™. Thanks to premium sound, hands-free voice control and a water/sweat-resistant build, these are truly your everyday, do-it-all earbuds. Features Skull-iQ Smart Voice Control technology to control audio, calls, Spotify Tap, and many other features hands-free (Compatible with smartphone devices on Android 10 or newer, and iOS 12 or newer)
features: Note : If the size of the earbud tips does not match the size of your ear canals or the headset is not worn properly in your ears, you may not obtain the correct sound qualities or call performance. Change the earbud tips to ones that fit more snugly in your ear

Supreme Sound - Relax, workout or run listening to your favorite tunes with crystal clear audio, gnarly bass and you'll be listening for up to 40 hours on a single charge. The case holds 31 hours, the buds hold 9 hours of battery
Skull-iQ Technology - Share your tunes with a friend in real time, activate your assistant, customize your earbud controls and trigger your phone camera to take the perfect selfie

Skull-iQ also offers complete hands free, meaning you never need to take off your gloves or let go of the handlebar. Play, pause, adjust volume, take calls, skip tracks, launch Spotify and activate Stay-Aware Mode, all without ever touching your device
Never lost with Tile - With Tile technology, Skullcandy tech makes it super easy to track down either earbud and keep your gadgets safe! Download the Tile app and follow the instructions to activate
Buy With Confidence - 1 Year warranty included with the Grind Fuel, whether you're an adult or child be sure to wear your earbuds in full confidence

reviews.title: Refurb- came coated with previous owner's ear wax - Yuck !  UPDATE !
reviews.text:
non hygenic refurb process disappoints- didn't try the super gross earbuds

UPDATE - I spent the extra $20 for a new set. these have issues. the earbuds turn on by themselves in the case and:
1.) hijack the phone connection
2.) use up all the battery, then charge up, and then run down the battery...so when you go to use the earbuds, the batteries are drained in earbuds and the case

other reviewers write of similar problem

skullcandy chat says they think the case needs to be replaced- I can send them in and wait a few weeks for their warranty repair. but it appears these issues are in the design

or return to amazon within the return period.

yes, that.

reviews.title: Nice sound

reviews.title: Stopped Charging
reviews.text: Stopped charging after a month. Return window just expired a few days ago. Now I have to deal with returning through Skullcandy

reviews.title: Comfort!
reviews.text: So the sound quality is good, battery life is good but for me the most important point is they are comfortable. I've been through a LOT of ear buds in search of something that I can wear for several hours without my ears starting to hurt and these are them! Charging case doesn't feel very substantial, but since I don't need to charge them every couple of hours I can live with it.

reviews.title: Interesting app
reviews.text: I've been using these earbuds for several weeks now, and they're as good as any pair I have. And I have several. They connect without issue and have good, clear sound. If you're looking for the intricasies of fine tuning, how good is the base and all that, I'm not the gal for you. However, I think music sounds great, and they're perfect for listening to YouTube vids on my computer and audio books on my phone. The app is interesting. At first I thought I could talk directly to my buds. "Hey, Skullcandy!" (First earbuds I've had with voice control.) But you have to use the app for that feature. Not quite as convenient in my opinion, although perhaps I was expecting too much. The controls are not touch, but require clicking on the individual buds to control them. You can also control them through the app, though. Instructions are clear and easy to understand. I was up and running in minutes. All in all, I think they are excellent and definitely worth the price tag.

title: Skullcandy Jib True Wireless Earbud with Microphone / 22 Hour Battery Life / Use with iPhone and Android / Best for Gym, and Gaming / Bluetooth Earbud Headphones - Black/Orange
description: Experience total freedom on a budget with Jib True. The newest true wireless earbud from Skullcandy is easy to connect and easy to enjoy. Call, track change, and volume controls are all within close reach through one main button. 22 hours of audio on a single charge means you spend less time recharging and more time enjoying your music. Jib True earbuds are adventure-ready, too. An IPX4 rating means Jib True is sweat- and dust-resistant. You don’t need to worry about damage if a little rain kicks up during your urban or outdoor adventure. Dual microphones make your calls more clear and if you feel like keeping one ear open, you can use either bud ‘solo’ without missing a beat.
features: Supreme Sound - Powered by 6mm drivers the Jib features dynamic audio with gnarly bass and a wide sound range. The earbuds also feature bluetooth 5.0 for a strong signal making sure you can listen to your music or take phone calls interruption free.

22 Hours of Play - With a 55mAh battery in the earbud and 500 mAh in the charging case you'll get a whopping 22 hours of charge total, 6 in the earbuds and 16 in the case. Use the included charging case for on the go charging as well as safe storage for your earbuds.

Dual Microphones - keeping you sounding extra clear while on calls or talking to your best friend Siri. Dual mics also means you can have 1 ear open and 1 earbud in while on calls to keep aware of your surroundings.

Small, Medium, Large Ears - Included in the Jib is 3 different eargel tips to make sure you have comfy noise isolating fit. The earbuds are super lightweight and water/dust resistant perfect for the active person!
Buy With Confidence - 1 year manufacturer's warranty included with the Jib, be sure to wear your earbuds with full confidence.

reviews.title: There is absolutely no bass, highs screeching high and nothing inbetween
reviews.text: These do not fit well and hard to keep in ear. My much cheaper earphones sound much better and much more bass. The treble is very high frequency but that is all you hear with this. Buy name brand and they would have more honest advertisement and better quality. I didn't expect to good bass for in ear earphones but this has non whatsoever and the high frequency could ruin your hearing. Maybe after few hundred hours it will sound better so will let you know. In the mean time pay a little more but get name brand.

reviews.title: Great sound, but a little bulky for an IEM
reviews.text: There are 6 drivers in here-1 DD and 5 BA, but the housings are pretty big. Definitely deep and will probably stick out of your ears, but sound great (with upgraded cable). For what you are paying, these IEMs are a steal. Sound fantastic. Find the the tips that work for you and upgrade the cable (I bought the 2.5 and 3.5 compatible cables from KBEAR and they are fantastic) and you will get as good of a sound as you will find from your source. Add a quality source (I use a Radsone EarStudio) and enjoy high quality sound on a budget.

reviews.title: Just What I Wanted
reviews.text: I wanted to replace my old clunker of a boom box.  I needed a small CD player with_ a speaker so I wouldn't have to wear ear buds.  That's what I found here. At first, I wondered about the sound, but considering how small it is, the sound is fine.  I'm glad to have easier access to my CD collection.

reviews.title: Great Bluetooth CD player
reviews.text:
I have a few CDs that I have kept despite mostly using streaming services. Music not available online. This device allows me to hear them again.
Connected very easy to my Bluetooth tailgate speaker.

Don't plan on using the built in speaker as I have several better speakers that I can connect to with cable or Bluetooth.

reviews.title: Sound Quality not the best on its own speakers
reviews.text:
I found using the Aux cable along with my Bluetooth headphones the sound quality is much better.
The headphones that came with unit wasn't that good.

reviews.title: Very Good Portable CD Player With Plenty of Options
reviews.text:
With the onset of Pandora, satellite radio and many other such options, I found I wasn’t playing my CDs anymore. In fact, I couldn’t find my CD player! And the high-end system in my truck doesn’t even have a CD option. So when I saw this I figured I give it a try. I like that it’s portable and plays without skipping even when bumped, running with it, or the dog knocking it off of the table. It can be used with my several different sets of earphones. It also plugs into my truck stereo without a problem.

I also wanted it to play through its own speaker while in my crafts room. This is where the 5th star gets removed. When turning it up nearly all the way the speaker starts to buzz on certain notes. It isn’t loud at all on its own either so using it without auxiliary speakers is not good if more than about 8 feet from it. However, I am playing my CDs which I have not heard in years, and that makes me very happy.

reviews.title: Fun and good
reviews.text:
My 13 yo son is on the vintage wave, so he has been using this and the cassette one. He is happy!
Very portable, small size.
The sound is ok.
Has been using it non stop and still working good.
Would be a fun gift for someone who likes old school (on a fun way, not like a collector)
We like it.

reviews.text: I’m just now ordering my second pair. Original pair still work, but the cord is finally stiff and cracking after thousands of miles cycling, hiking, and hundreds of hours in the gym. I see a lot of people complaining about the mic, I don’t think I’ve ever used that function so I can’t speak to that.  However, the sound quality is better than my iPod Pros, and they still sound great at 25mph on the bike, I’ve never once had an issue with wind noise or of them falling out.  Great value for the money.

reviews.title: The Moondrop Chu is dead. Long live the new king.
reviews.text:
Well the Moondrop Chu's reign didn't last long. These WILDLY outclass everything under $50. They wildly outclass a lot of stuff under $200.

For starters, detachable cable. I hate the fixed cable on the moondrop chu's with a passion - it's all silicone rubbery and tangles, doesn't stay in place, doesn't stay tied up in your pocket or backpack. The chu's come with a microphone, which is the only thing it has going for it, but if you REALLY need a cable, these are still a far better deal with another $10 spent on the cable. This cable is nice, comfortable, wraps up easily around 3-4 fingers and stays coiled properly.

Then, eartips. The Chu's "springtips" don't really fit quite a few people, myself included. They were so bad that the chu's sounded downright broken until I got some better tips on them. The Zero's come with about five different size of tips that were no problem to find a comfortable seal/fit.

Comfort and build wise, they're awesome. My ears can be quite picky, and they basically disappear in your head. They're light and smooth plastic, with a nice stainless steel plate on the outside. No complaints, they just work perfectly as IEM's and feel plenty durable to just live in your backpack.

Sound wise, these things really up the game. The Chu's have excellent technical performance (detail is great) but they're downright claustrophobic. It feels like every instrument in the band is licking the inside of your ear. And it's unpleasant. They're tuned quite neutral, but with no sense of space, instrument separation or imaging (that sense of more than left/right, but front/back and in between) they're just not a pleasant sound. The Zero's have IMPRESSIVE separation and imaging. Not impressive at $20. Impressive for IEM's, period. There's $300-500 stuff that doesn't do imaging and soundstage this well.

The bass could be cleaner and it doesn't quite hit the detail of the chu's (which are also punching well into the $500 range in that one specific area) but I'm nitpicking HARD. For $20, these have no right sounding so spacious, detailed (you can enjoy the detail a lot more when the instruments don't feel all laid on top of each other) and pleasant. They're not overly bassy, but they are thumpier than the chu's. No overly sparkly either. Just a very "grown up" tuning meant to make music sound nice, paired with truly outstanding soundstage.

If you play video games, there's nothing anywhere near these in the price range. You'll hear sounds ahead, behind, and how far away they are.

Songs with real detail and separation will dance around you (Bobby McFerrin, Polyphia) and show you things you didn't even know were in your music.

The only next step up I can think of from these is the $50 Truthear zero's (same model name, different brand) but they're only marginally better (with a slightly worse fit) and the $100 7hz diokos are a very noticeable step up in every way. But jeez. These things are $20. I don't understand how they're so good for that little money.

Anyone else entering the $20-$50 space better bring their A+ game. These are a tough act to follow. If you really need a microphone, these are still unbeatable with another $10-15 for a cable with one.

reviews.title: Fantastic First IEM w/ Insane Value
reviews.text:
These were my first entry into IEMs and I really enjoyed the experience. Coming from Google Pixel Buds Pros, these were a significant improvement in sound quality but given the abundance of excellent budget headphones in the chi-fi market, I can't comment as to whether they're better than any of the others you'd find for a similar price.

I also really like the USB-C 2-pin cable that it comes with. It has a subtler finish compared to twisted cables and it also has a built-in mic that works well enough for calls. I actually bought a higher end set of IEMS and ended up using the Salnotes' cable since similar cables cost 1-2x the price of the Salnotes themselves.

reviews.title: Cheap Buys Cheap
reviews.text: Not sure what I expected for so little, so shame on me. I'll tell you these made my 9-year-old's ears hurt. I'm not sure how they could cancel any noise, as there is no buffer. I tried the ones she put on and they are very uncomfortable. Returning.

reviews.title: Great product and great price.
reviews.text: We bought a pack of these for my son because he goes through headphones crazy fast; since we bought these, he's still on the first pair. We will be buying more in the future if we have to.

reviews.title: They are priced low enough that a defective or marginal one doesn't sour the price.
reviews.text: Quality control could be a little better, but the price for the multipack is tough to beat.

reviews.title: For the cost… I am super happy with them.
reviews.text: I’m giving these earbuds five stars because they exceed my expectations when they only cost seventy cents each. I find it hard to complain about these, especially considering the cost. The sound is quite adequate for such earbuds. I use these in a classroom setting for kids who either cannot afford earbuds or forget there is at home. They are individually packaged. Nice assortment of colors. Though they are being sold by a US company, I do not show where they are made. I can only assume they are made in China.

reviews.title: Great Sound Just Wish they had a Microphone
reviews.text: Fun colors and love this type of ear plug over the cone shaped style. Listening to music on my phone sounds great  but I wish they had a microphone so I could easily answer calls. Now I have to unplug the earphones when the phone starts to ring and then re-plug after I'm done on the call. I guess they are really designed for use on exclusive music players. The listing is confusing because it says "Bluetooth" which they don't have, but the wire is about 4ft. long so your music unit can easily be kept anywhere convenient. No volume control either so that has to be handled on your phone/music player.

title: PALOVUE Wireless Earbuds, in-Ear Earphones with Bluetooth 5.3, Built-in Mic, 35H Playtime Deep Bass Stereo, with Lightweight Compact Charging Case for Sport/Work Compatible iPhone Android
features: 【Reliable Bluetooth 5.3】PALOVUE wireless earbuds with cutting-edge Bluetooth 5.3 innovation provide instant and steady transmission, providing you with a continuous calling and music experience as well as noise cancellation.
【Automatic Pairing】When removed from the charging case, the wireless headphones would be moment-matched and auto-reconnect with the last-matched device.

【Ergonomic Lightweight Design】The ergonomic in-ear design of the headphones fits in each ear and comes with four different types of silicone ear tips to accommodate every ear profile for a pleasant wearing experience. They weigh 4g apiece and can be worn effectively without pressure, while the charging case weighs 28g and can easily fit in your pocket and be hefted around, making it ideal for commuting, working out, and travelling, among other things.

【Clear HD Sound】The Bluetooth codec supports AAC/SBC and is designed with premium speakers, providing you powerful highs and profound bass and allowing you to enjoy superb music. They include two built-in microphones for clear and raucous calls, making them suitable for any scenario, such as webinars and online classes.

【Waterproof Smart Control】Equipped with IPX5 waterproof grade protects the headphones from perspiration during workout. Ideal for cycling, jogging, exercise, skiing, touring, trekking, and other outdoor activities. Also enables dual-ear easy touch volume/music/call/voice assistant control, among other things, and can switch between single and binaural modes at will. Reliable product quality combined with powerful technology and 2-years after-sales service.

reviews.title: They bump!
reviews.text: These are amazing! For the price, they bump, and now canceling for sure! Nice small size not bulky case. Many sizes to fit many people included. I love them already! Functionality of tapping seems really good also. I was able to move them in my ear without interruption of accidental tap. Very pleased.

title: Klipsch AS-5i In-Ear Headphones
features: Connectivity Technology: Wired
Extremely durable, tangle-resistant cabling withstands pulls, snags, etc.
Small driver design delivers big sound that covers a wide frequency range with dynamic detail.
Soft, flexible oval ear tips naturally fit the contours of the ear canal for comfortable, long-term wear and create a perfect acoustic seal for superior noise isolation.

reviews.title: Klipsch AS51i blue all sporting-ear headphones
reviews.text: These things sound great.

reviews.title: These are crap ear buds don't get them.
reviews.text: These are not loud at all. I can barely hear what I'm playing through them in bed, at the gym it's impossible to make anything out. You'd figure that spending this much on ear buds would mean they'd be good, high quality ear buds, but that simply is not the case.

reviews.title: but the reality is that I love this brand with speakers
reviews.text:
Time has passed since I returned them, so I don't remember exactly all the details, but the reality is that I love this brand with speakers, my main left and right speakers in my home theater are Klipsch. Excellent. But these in-ear headphones sounded bad. No low end, mid-rangy. Only manually pushing them I could get a better sound, but always too much towards the mids and mid-high frequencies.
I had higher expectations with them, knowing the brand. I thought they could be headphones I would like and trust to listen to music, and also being able to do exercises. I'm not 100% sure now, but I think I remember I couldn't keep them on while running and sweating.

reviews.title: Great Sound With Comfort And Stability
reviews.text:
This is an excellent set of Sports headphones that sounds great and stays put when you're getting your workout in. The headphones use a dual magnet, dynamic moving coil micro speaker to deliver a frequency response of 10HZ to 19kHz making them more than adequate for listening outside of the gym as well. While these headphones work with any device they are truly made for the Apple line of products (iPhone, iPad, iPod) as the control box gives you control over the volume, skip tracks and even take calls.

►► SOUND QUALITY ◄◄

The sound across the board is excellent from the low-end through the mid-range and high-end. They are not as open and airy as my Ultimate Ears TripleFi 10's, a set that listed for $399 retail, but they don't feel muted at all when listening to them.

Testing through a variety of music genres it would seem that these are tuned for the louder, upbeat music that you'd listen to while working out which makes sense since these are Sports headphones. With that said if I would have no problem listening to acoustic music through these as they product a nicely balanced, clean sound that is better than a lot of wired headsets I've tried. They are way better than the headphones included with my iPhone.

►► COMFORT ◄◄

For me comfort is just as important as sound quality because if your in-ear monitors aren't comfortable you won't want to wear them for extended periods of time. This set does a couple things right in the comfort arena with the design of the oval earbuds and the over the ear wire design that keeps the headphones in place and makes them feel as close to weightless as you can get.

I was a bit skeptical of the oval design of the earbuds as I am very used to the circular design and have been very happy with the Comply tips that I normally use. With that said the oval design here truly does seem to make a difference in the comfort of these tips as the included tips are more comfortable than most, if not all, of the standard tips I've had included in a set of headphones.

►► CONTROLS ◄◄

There is a small control box attached to the clip that you can use to keep the cord in place. The control box is very similar to those on other sets of headphones I have tested and it worked pretty much as expected except for the way you move up and down in the track list.

The top button on the control box is used to raise the volume on your device.

The bottom button on the control box is used to lower the volume on your device.

The center button is the most versatile as a quick click will play or pause your music or answer a call if the phone is ringing. A double tap of the center button will move to the next track in your track listing and a triple tap of the center button will move to the previous track. Pressing and holding the center button for a second will initiate Siri. While I am used to the volume buttons normally doubling up as the previous / next track control once I was used to this system it was a breeze to use.

►► PHONE CALLS ◄◄

The headset does a nice job with calls due to the fact that the microphone is nice and clear so there is no worries about taking a call while wearing these headphones.

►► CONCLUSION ◄◄

Overall this is an excellent set of Sports headphones that are both comfortable and sound great. They stay firmly in place and seem to be built to handle whatever wear and tear you will throw at them while working out. I love having full control over my iPhone with the included control. For me this is about as close to perfect as you can come with a Sports set of headphones. Highly recommended.

Sample provided for review.

reviews.title: Just right for me.
reviews.text: Diggin' these just fine. I can see how maybe people with tiny ears, or oddly shaped ears, might not have the right fit. Also, the portion in-ear is quite tiny, so there's not a huge amount of bass, but I still like the clarity, and there's enough adjustability to tailor the sound, even on my tiny Sandisc Mp3 player. What I like the most though, is the over the ear design, and I can have the wires feed out back out of my way. The portion in-ear, with the smallish oval tips, also makes me quite happy. So small, and un-noticeable, and comfortable, and they stay put till the end of my hike, or run, or skate, or whatever. The ones that came with the player sounded good too, but really annoying and would fall out when you get sweaty or turn your head. And I just didn't have a bunch of the new-style design to try out, with the horn on them that you rotate and lock into the ear. I read that those kind still fall out when sweaty and slippery anyway. (or you don't get the right fit). All problems solved with these sporty little guys. And Klipsch speakers and equipment have always impressed, so I'm glad I trusted my instincts...

reviews.title: $1 sound
